hero.tagline: . Focused on Java/Spring and Python. Studying architecture to understand how systems really work.

about.label: Who I am
about.title: About me
about.p1: I'm in the 6th semester of Software Engineering at UniBH and I work as a development intern at Group Software, where I use Java, Spring Boot, Angular and Flutter on real condominium management systems.
about.p2: My focus is on Java/Spring and Python. I study software architecture because I want to understand the underlying processes — not just make things work, but understand why they work.
about.p3Start: Outside of work, I build my own projects that I use every day.
about.p3Highlight: Equilibra, my personal finance system, is what best represents who I am as a developer.

experience.label: Path
experience.title: Experience & Education
experience.groupDetail: Building and maintaining ERP systems for condominium management using Java and Spring Boot on the backend. Developing and evolving interfaces with Angular and TypeScript. Mobile development with Flutter for the building manager app. Real product environment with code in production from day one.
experience.unibhDetail: Studies focused on software development, systems architecture, databases, and requirements engineering.

skills.label: Skills
skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: What I use at work and on projects — focused on Java/Spring and Python.

projects.label: Portfolio
projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: What I built, why I built it, and what I used.

equilibra.description: Personal finance system that replaced my spreadsheet.
equilibra.long: Accounts, credit cards, invoices, transactions and investments — all in one place with real business rules. My family and I use it daily. Completely free.
highlights: Lazy invoice creation — generated automatically on the first transaction of the month, no pre-allocation
Inline ghost closing — invoice status updated on query, without relying on a scheduled job
Daily scheduler with ShedLock — protected against duplicate execution across multiple instances
Optimistic Locking with @Version on accounts, cards and invoices — no pessimistic locks
Integration tests with Testcontainers and real PostgreSQL — no database mocks

aican.long: Integration with Google Gemini to generate workouts and nutrition suggestions based on the user's physical data and preferences. Free APIs.
highlights: Asynchronous backend with FastAPI (Python 3.10+) and SQLAlchemy
Intelligent workout generation using Google Gemini 2.0 Flash API
Abuse prevention and security with Rate Limiting via SlowAPI

mapa.description: Interactive navigation system for the university campus.
mapa.long: Route calculation, location search, WhatsApp sharing and light/dark theme. Built to help freshmen and visitors find their way.
highlights: Interactive campus visualization with Leaflet.js and OpenStreetMap
Dynamic route calculation using OpenRouteService API
Node.js backend deployed on Render and frontend hosted on Vercel
